SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.safety.hazid

Class HazopConsequenceFinding

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.hazid.HazopConsequenceFinding
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public final class HazopConsequenceFinding extends Object implements Serializable
A single quantified HAZOP consequence finding produced by HazopConsequenceAutoPopulator.quantify(neqsim.process.processmodel.ProcessSystem, HazopQuantificationLimits).

Where the static catalogue in HazopConsequenceAutoPopulator only states which NeqSim calculator applies to a guide-word/parameter deviation, a finding carries the computed number from running that calculation against the live flowsheet state, the design limit it was compared against, and a deterministic HazopConsequenceFinding.Verdict. This is the part of the HAZOP that an annotation- or rule-based P&ID checker cannot produce on its own — the consequence value comes from the thermodynamic engine (for example, a compressor discharge temperature from polytropic compression plus a flash, or a valve outlet temperature from an isenthalpic Joule-Thomson flash).

Findings are immutable. They are intended as screening evidence for a HAZOP facilitator and a competent review team, not as a finished HAZOP record.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • HazopConsequenceFinding

      public HazopConsequenceFinding(String nodeId, String unitName, HAZOPTemplate.GuideWord guideWord, HAZOPTemplate.Parameter parameter, double computedValue, double designLimit, String valueUnit, HazopConsequenceFinding.Verdict verdict, String calculator, String standardReference, String message)
      Construct a quantified HAZOP consequence finding without an explicit limit basis. The limit basis defaults to "not specified"; prefer the twelve-argument constructor so the verdict carries an auditable explanation of which policy threshold was applied.
      Parameters:
      nodeId - the HAZOP node identifier, in the same format as HAZOPTemplate.fromProcessSystem(neqsim.process.processmodel.ProcessSystem)
      unitName - the name of the unit operation the finding relates to
      guideWord - the HAZOP guide-word of the deviation
      parameter - the HAZOP parameter of the deviation
      computedValue - the consequence value computed from the flowsheet state; NaN when not evaluated
      designLimit - the design limit the consequence was compared against; NaN when not evaluated
      valueUnit - the unit of both the computed value and the design limit, for example "C"
      verdict - the deterministic verdict
      calculator - a short description of the NeqSim calculation that produced the value
      standardReference - the governing standard reference, for example "API 617 / API 521"
      message - a human-readable explanation of the finding

    • HazopConsequenceFinding

      public HazopConsequenceFinding(String nodeId, String unitName, HAZOPTemplate.GuideWord guideWord, HAZOPTemplate.Parameter parameter, double computedValue, double designLimit, String valueUnit, HazopConsequenceFinding.Verdict verdict, String calculator, String standardReference, String message, String limitBasis)
      Construct a quantified HAZOP consequence finding with an explicit limit basis.
      Parameters:
      nodeId - the HAZOP node identifier, in the same format as HAZOPTemplate.fromProcessSystem(neqsim.process.processmodel.ProcessSystem)
      unitName - the name of the unit operation the finding relates to
      guideWord - the HAZOP guide-word of the deviation
      parameter - the HAZOP parameter of the deviation
      computedValue - the consequence value computed from the flowsheet state; NaN when not evaluated
      designLimit - the design limit the consequence was compared against; NaN when not evaluated
      valueUnit - the unit of both the computed value and the design limit, for example "C"
      verdict - the deterministic verdict
      calculator - a short description of the NeqSim calculation that produced the value
      standardReference - the governing standard reference, for example "API 617 / API 521"
      message - a human-readable explanation of the finding
      limitBasis - an auditable explanation of where the design limit came from (default value, per-unit override, equipment data sheet, governing standard), so the green/red verdict is traceable

      the computed value, or NaN when the finding was not evaluated

      Gets the design limit the consequence was compared against.
      Returns:
      the design limit, or NaN when the finding was not evaluated

      Gets the unit of the computed value and design limit.
      Returns:

      Gets the deterministic verdict.
      Returns:
      the verdict

    • exceedsLimit

      public boolean exceedsLimit()
      Indicates whether the computed consequence breaches the design limit.
      Returns:
      true if the verdict is HazopConsequenceFinding.Verdict.EXCEEDS

    • getCalculator

      public String getCalculator()
      Gets the short description of the NeqSim calculation that produced the value.
      Returns:
      the calculator description

    • getStandardReference

      Gets the governing standard reference.
      Returns:
      the standard reference

    • getMessage

      public String getMessage()
      Gets the human-readable explanation of the finding.
      Returns:
      the message

    • getLimitBasis

      public String getLimitBasis()
      Gets the auditable basis for the design limit used in this finding.
      Returns:
      an explanation of where the design limit came from (default, per-unit override, data sheet, standard)
